params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 6 to get the latest

params.name: shipid
params.description:
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi

params.name: mmsi
params.description: The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880

params.name: movetype
params.description: Use 0 to only receive arrivals or 1 to only receive departures. If not used, the response will include both

params.name: exclude_intransit
params.description: Use 1 to exclude vessels in transit

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Portcalls between fromdate and todate. Maximum period requested 190 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM

params.name: todate
params.description: Portcalls between fromdate and todate. Maximum period requested 190 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM

params.name: dwt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: dwt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: gt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: gt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: market
params.description: Define market(s) for which you would like to receive port calls (single or comma separated values) - more

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes voyage related data since the previous port call

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

items.docs:
Retrieve arrival and departure information of a vessel for a specific period of time.

Notes
  • Data is available for dates **after** 2015-01-16
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data
  • **MOVE_TYPE** values: 1=Departure, 0=Arrival
  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed in your

A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi

params.name: todate

params.name: event_type
params.description: You may use one or more event ids in comma separated format for the events you wish to receive

params.name: limit_events
params.description: Limit the number of results

items.docs:
Get event based intelligence for a vessel.

Notes
  • Here is a COMPLETE LIST OF EVENT TYPES available for this endpoint. You can use these as values for the **event_type** query parameter.
  • Information about AIS-transmitted data

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

params.name: shipid
params.description:
A uniquely assigned ID by MarineTraffic for the subject vessel

You can instead use imo or mmsi

params.name: mmsi
params.description: The Maritime Mobile Service Identity (MMSI) of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: imo
params.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) number of the vessel you wish to track

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or greater than provided fromdate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

params.name: todate
params.description: Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or less than provided todate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

params.name: market
params.description:
Define market for which you would like to receive berth calls - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then return all
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, return based on ship-class
  • if defined and ship-class is undefined, return all ship-classes of the specific market

params.name: shipclass
params.description: Define ship class of interest - more

params.name: dwt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)

params.name: dwt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)

params.name: gt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: gt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: movetype
params.description:
Use 0 to include both, 1 to only receive dockings or 2 for only undockings within the defined period. If not used, only dockings are returned

Available only from version 2 of the service

params.name: protocol
params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:
  • xml
  • csv
  • jsono

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
  • simple
  • extended
If used with the value extended, the response includes voyage related data since the previous port call

  • Information about AIS-transmitted data

  • The frequency of allowed API calls is specific to your API key and is detailed

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key: 40-character hexadecimal number

params.name: v
params.description: Version of the service to be executed. Use version 3 to get the latest

params.name: portid
params.description:
The MarineTraffic ID of the port you wish to monitor (found on the URL of the respective Port page)

You can instead use port_unlocode, berthid or terminalid

params.name: port_unlocode
params.description: Port UN LOCODE (without gaps or spaces)

params.name: berthid
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the berth you wish to monitor (list to be provided upon request)

params.name: terminalid
params.description: The MarineTraffic ID of the terminal you wish to monitor (list to be provided upon request)

params.name: timespan
params.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the returned port calls. Maximum value is 2880

params.name: fromdate
params.description: Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or greater than provided fromdate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.

params.name: todate
params.description: Return Berth Calls where DOCK_TIMESTAMP_UTC is equal to or less than provided todate. Maximum period requested 15 days </br></br> Date format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS

params.name: market
params.description:
Define market for which you would like to receive berth calls - more
  • if undefined and ship-class is also undefined, then return all
  • if undefined and ship-class is defined, return based on ship-class
  • if defined and ship-class is undefined, return all ship-classes of the specific market

params.name: shipclass
params.description: Define ship class of interest - more

params.name: dwt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)

params.name: dwt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum DWT </br></br> Use it to filter by size Cargo and Tanker IMO-having vessels (shiptype = 7,8)

params.name: gt_min
params.description: Data filter: minimum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: gt_max
params.description: Data filter: maximum GT </br></br> Applicable to IMO-having vessels

params.name: movetype
params.description:
Use 0 to include both, 1 to only receive dockings or 2 for only undockings within the defined period. If not used, only dockings are returned

Available only from version 2 of the service

params.description:
Response type. Use one of the following:

  • jsono

params.name: msgtype
params.description:
Resolution of the response. Available values:
